title: The Islamic Ruling on Masturbation?
stanceSummary: Yasir Qadhi addresses "The Islamic Ruling on Masturbation" and clarifies the ruling with its limits, evidence, and real-world application. He emphasizes following foundational Islamic principles while recognizing context, hardship, and scholarly nuance when applying the ruling.
alternateQuestions: Is the islamic ruling on masturbation halal or haram in Islam?
What is the Islamic ruling on the islamic ruling on masturbation?
What does Yasir Qadhi say about the islamic ruling on masturbation?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020-10-07 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nr9AI-OAYhg) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nr9AI-OAYhg&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi discusses the ruling, its evidence, and practical caveats.

- **Question:** The Islamic Ruling on Masturbation?
- **Method:** The answer is framed through Quranic principles, Sunnah-based reasoning, and classical fiqh discussion.
- **Practical guidance:** The discussion distinguishes between the general rule and exceptional circumstances.

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### Question and Context — [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nr9AI-OAYhg&t=0)

Of them so the first question we're going to be doing insha'allah is uh the permissibility of self-stimulation or masturbation is it permissible in Islam or is this act sinful m generally speaking uh the majority of scholars the majority of madhhabs view a masturbation as being haram or sinful and they base this on the fact that Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala forbids all sexual activity except with one's spouse and Allah says in the Quran that uh whoever um that Allah praises those who protect their private parts except.

Allah says in the Quran except with their spouses or their right hand possessions whoever is within this confines they are they are that is permissible uh whoever goes beyond this then they have transgressed now imam shafiri explicitly used this verse to say that self-stimulation is therefore not allowed because according to him this is going beyond the verse this is uh the most explicit verse that has been used to forbid masturbation there is nothing else in the Quran that forbids it and there is no authentic hadith.

There are a number of very weak and actually fabricated a hadith about masturbation but there is nothing explicit that is authentic about masturbation now imam shafer he did derive this from the verse but again the verse doesn't explicitly mention self-stimulation and it is correct to point out that this is the mainstream position of the shafi'i school and of the maliki school and of the majority of the hanafi school as well however some of the scholars of the past they did consider it to be and others.

Considered to be mubah so there are three primary positions in this regard the first is that it is actually sinful so it would be basically within the realm of haram the second is that it is makru and makruh means that it is not sinful but it is better not to do and this is the position that i advocate and the third is that it is completely mubah there is not even a discouragement it is not even something that is against nobility it is completely permissible so.

These are the three positions out there and as for the position that it is mubah that it is completely permissible it is one of the positions of the Hanbali school the Hanbali school has other positions as well it is one of the positions of the Hanbali school it is also the position of imam ashokani the famous scholar of yemen and as for the position that it is makru then it is reported by a number of tabirun and one can derive this as well from the statements.

Of some of the sahaba as we will mention and it is also the position of ibn hasem the famous scholar of andalus that he said it is not sinful but it is better not to do which translates as makruh so a number of quotations here uh to understand that this is not something that is uh fringe opinion it is something that is well known the famous Hanbali scholar al mardawi uh he mentions that if one resorts to masturbation out of a fear of falling into fornication.

It is mubah in fact it goes so far as to say that it might even be obligatory so that he avoids the the bigger sin of actual zina of actual uh intercourse that is uh haram and he also mentions very explicitly that the same ruling applies for a woman that a woman can also resort to this if there is a fear of falling into zina and there are a number of reports from the sahaba and tabirun that indicate that this position was found amongst them as.

This to save themselves from zina okay so the tabiri mujahid he mentions that that this is something that was known that instead of committing zina if it's very if the emotions or the lust is very powerful and one feels that they're going to fall into haram then they should do this.
